name: Mode Analytics API
what_it_does: REST API for Mode Analytics enabling programmatic management of SQL reports, queries, data sources, spaces, and report runs for data team collaboration workflows.
use_cases: Automating report runs and refreshes on schedule or trigger
Programmatically creating and managing SQL reports
Integrating Mode report results into other tools and workflows
Managing data source connections and permissions at scale
Building report catalogs and organizing spaces programmatically
not_for: Teams that need embedded analytics in customer-facing products
best_when: Your data team already uses Mode for SQL analytics and you need to automate report scheduling, extract results, or integrate Mode reports into other workflows
avoid_when: You need embedded customer-facing analytics, real-time data, or are evaluating BI platforms from scratch

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using API token and API secret. Tokens generated per user in Mode account settings. No OAuth, no scopes, no service accounts.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for individual analysts with limited queries and data sources
pricing.paid_tiers: Business and Enterprise tiers with team features; pricing not publicly listed
pricing.notes: API access available on Business and Enterprise plans. Free tier has API access with significant limitations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes with JSON bodies; report run errors include query-level detail in run results
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report runs create new run instances; no idempotency keys on creation endpoints
known_agent_gotchas: Report runs are async; callers must poll run status before fetching results
API tokens are user-scoped; no service account pattern available
Rate limit of 100 req/min can be hit quickly when polling run status
Report results returned as paginated CSV or JSON but format must be explicitly requested
Mode is acquired by ThoughtSpot; API roadmap and SLA uncertain

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; Basic Auth over HTTPS; tokens are long-lived with no built-in rotation mechanism; SOC2 Type II certified
